programmes.show_description: A Sinister Attachment. Daniel and Claire believe renovations on their home have stirred a residual energy. The team investigate whether Claire has a demonic attachment. S3, Ep1
The Grey Lady Of Sinai. When Kate asks for help with her 14th century hunting lodge, the team discover a mind-blowing theory and the sinister story of a murdered woman. S3, Ep2
Teleshopping.
Taking Risks. Drew's business has expanded so he needs twice as many items! He visits a quirky house in Suffolk, to discover a barn full of salvage from around the globe. S12, Ep5
Moving With The Times. When other businesses move with the times, Drew sees a chance to buy their unwanted stuff. In Essex, he finds a rare 1930s desk from a closing department store. S12, Ep6
Gaby Roslin helps punk-pop princess Toyah Willcox search for a second home in the leafy London area of Richmond upon Thames. She has £650,000 to spend. S2, Ep2
Your one-stop-shop for all things cruise; bringing you the hottest destinations, ground-breaking ships and the latest deals to ensure your next cruise is your greatest one!
Prospective buyers around the UK wanting to swap hectic city living for a home in the country are shown an array of beautiful rural properties. S17, Ep28
Holland. Drew heads to Holland where, thanks to its thriving antiques markets, he can find the kind of unique pieces he's renowned for - including his dream car, a vintage Bugatti. S12, Ep8
Drew unearths a steel corpus of Christ on the cross, and spots a divine early 19th century gothic pew. Later, he finds a set of 1950s industrial pendant lamps. S12, Ep9
In Gloucestershire, Drew finds a huge horse's head with a dealer specialising in sculptures. And, in Somerset he spots a valuable Persian rug lining a chicken shed. S12, Ep10
Tim Medhurst and Catherine Southon hunt for profitable items in Brimfield. Catherine eyes bronze swimming medals, while Tim spends big on a mule saddle. S19, Ep9
Antiques experts Catherine Southon and Tim Medhurst embark on the last leg of their road trip. Who will be victorious at the final auction in Devizes? S19, Ep10
Toy restorers Julia and Amanda repair a worn and cracked 1970s puppet. Clockmaker Steve brings a Congreve rolling ball clock back to its former glory. S6, Ep26
Record-Breaking Auction. A couple have no idea how valuable their huge haul of gold coins, watches and jewellery is. And a couple have some rare masonic regalia to sell. S2, Ep3

programmes.show_name: Long Lost Family: What Happened Next
programmes.show_description: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell revisit families they helped reunite to find out what has happened since and how their lives have been transformed. S5, Ep2

programmes.show_name: Long Lost Family: What Happened Next
programmes.show_description: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell revisit families they helped reunite to find out what has happened since and how their lives have been transformed. S5, Ep3
Tongs Will Wag! Quirky items, including a secret society's ballot box, an uncle's gold tooth, and expensive kitchenware, create a buzz in the auction room. S6, Ep6
He Who Dares Sells. Angus helps an RAF hero sell a rare SAS diary to raise cash for veterans. Also, a man parts with 250 cars, hoping to fund the cruise of a lifetime. S6, Ep7
Antiques experts travel the UK with a budget of £200 each in a competition to find and buy antiques or rare items and sell them for a profit. S25, Ep15
Fix This Rotted Rancher. Dave and Jenny take on a reno close to home, but devastating structural rot threatens the entire plan - and there's a heartbreaking personal loss. S7, Ep9
The Wedding. An engaged couple update a mid-century home for their blended family, but setbacks have Ben and Erin racing to finish before the wedding. S9, Ep2
The Ghost Of Gertie. The Bull Hotel dates back to the 14th century and is said to be a hotbed of paranormal activity. The team investigate a child spirit that's been scaring staff. S3, Ep3
Possessive Petts Lane. Krissie blames a possessive spirit for the break-up of two relationships. Could the spirit be Samuel Pett, who lived in her home in the 18th century? S3, Ep4
Murder At The Museum. The team investigate True's Yard Fisherfolk Museum. There have been reports of ghostly sightings, strange noises and a violent child spirit. S3, Ep5
Teleshopping.
Holland. Drew heads to Holland where, thanks to its thriving antiques markets, he can find the kind of unique pieces he's renowned for - including his dream car, a vintage Bugatti. S12, Ep8
Drew unearths a steel corpus of Christ on the cross, and spots a divine early 19th century gothic pew. Later, he finds a set of 1950s industrial pendant lamps. S12, Ep9
Gaby Roslin helps Kelly Hoppen, interior designer to the rich and famous, find a rural retreat in the Oxfordshire countryside. She has £3million to spend. S2, Ep3
Your one-stop-shop for all things cruise; bringing you the hottest destinations, ground-breaking ships and the latest deals to ensure your next cruise is your greatest one!
Drew goes to the biggest antiques emporium he's ever visited and a Somerset antique shop. At a grand school in Ipswich he uncovers a historical artifact that leaves him speechless. S10, Ep10
At a luxury fabric manufacturer in Suffolk, Drew is wowed by historic silks once used in the royal household. And at a castle in Scotland, he haggles with a young baron. S10, Ep11
Anneka Rice & Antony Cotton. Angus clears the family home of TV icon Anneka Rice and looks at a collection of quirky items at soap star Antony Cotton's Lancashire cottage. S2, Ep1
In search of treasure, Angus visits the stunning home of former royal correspondent Jennie Bond and clears soap legend Sid Owen's idyllic French retreat. S2, Ep2
Ann Widdecombe & Owain Wyn Evans. Angus uncovers model kits at the home of weatherman Owain Wyn Evans and clears the Devonshire bungalow of retired politician Ann Widdecombe. S2, Ep3
Student Takes On Master. Debut road tripper Hettie Jago takes on regular dealer David Harper. The pair start in Scotland with £1500 each and a 1980s Mercedes 280 SL. S27, Ep11
A Close Encounter With A Cow. Auctioneer Hettie Jago takes on dealer David Harper. The second leg of their trip takes them to Glasgow and the Scottish Borders. S27, Ep12
It's All Kicking Off. Hettie, David and their 1980s Mercedes 280 SL are in Cumbria. But who will strike a decisive blow in their best-of-five auction contest? S27, Ep13
Horses, Crabs And Sausages. Hettie and David hope that their 1980s Mercedes SL 280 convertible will cope with the challenging roads of the 'District' as they hunt for antiques. S27, Ep14
Auction Showdown! It's the end of the tour for David Harper and Hettie Jago, as they head towards their final auction. Who will be crowned the winner? S27, Ep15
EJ Osborne attempts to revamp a Lloyd Loom-style chair. Daniel Heath works on a pair of bookcases, while Norman Wilkinson transforms a pine table. S7, Ep17
Bruce Faulseit is challenged to transform an old oak table. Neil Wragg tries to repurpose an inflatable bed, while Sarah Moore fixes up a Victorian sofa. S7, Ep18
Jay Blades sees hidden potential in a collection of carpet offcuts, a pair of tired chairs and a bicycle wheel. How will these discarded items be transformed? S7, Ep19
To Son, With Love. A devoted grandma won $1,000,000 on a quick pick. Now she wants her youngest son and his family to find their own home in Bridgeport, Connecticut. S12, Ep5
Seven Million Big Ones. Despite winning $7,000,000, two New York EMTs want to save most of it for travelling the globe after they retire. David helps them find a Florida dream home. S12, Ep6
Vision To Reality. Brian and Mika help newlyweds aiming to turn their dream farmhouse vision board into a reality. It features the biggest closet they've ever built! S5, Ep3
Allison Janney's Showstopper. Allison Janney wants to surprise her assistant. With Drew and Jonathan's help, they flip her kitchen and dining room to create a stylish space. S2, Ep4
